task: The input to HADDOCK3 consists of atomic coordinates of biomolecules (proteins, glycans, etc.) in PDB format, along with optional experimental restraints (e.g., ambiguous interaction restraints) and user-defined workflows. The output is an ensemble of modeled three-dimensional structures of biomolecular complexes, ranked and clustered according to various scoring functions. The scientific goal is to provide a versatile, modular platform for integrative modeling that leverages experimental data to predict accurate structures of biomolecular complexes, complementing machine learning approaches.

data.name: 1brs_AD.pdb
data.description: Processed structure of barnase-barstar complex (chains A and D) without water, used as input for HADDOCK3 analysis.

data.name: skempi_v2.csv
data.description: SKEMPI 2.0 database containing experimental binding affinity changes upon mutation, used for validation.
